containerPurpose: Portable input container for Antigravity or another AI IDE to generate or extend a simple service-business Next.js website.

implementationTarget.initialDeliverable: One-page splash page with branding and a lead form

doUse: White header and high-contrast dark hero/footer sections
Three service cards overlapping the hero
doNotUse: Do not copy Seek Comfort content
Do not lock the layout to HVAC
Do not use Seek Comfort colors directly
Do not use low-contrast white text on pale backgrounds

integrationPlan.currentFormMode: The form posts to src/app/api/lead/route.ts. It sends through Resend when RESEND_API_KEY, LEAD_TO_EMAIL, and LEAD_FROM_EMAIL are configured. Without those env vars, it returns a local demo-mode success message.
integrationPlan.nextAdapter: Use Resend first unless the client specifically requests SMTP, GoDaddy SMTP, or Google Workspace SMTP/OAuth.
integrationPlan.recommendedAbstraction: Keep provider secrets in environment variables. If future providers are added, split provider-specific logic into src/lib/lead-adapters and call it from the route handler.

launchPlan.preferredOwnership: Client-owned Vercel account or team and client-owned Resend account.
launchPlan.temporaryOption: Tony can deploy under his Vercel account for speed, point DNS to that deployment, then transfer the project later.
launchPlan.emailDnsCaution: Do not remove existing MX records if Gmail/GoDaddy is currently handling domain email. Add only the DNS records required by Vercel and Resend.
